title: Apterygia conferta
taxonomy: Apterygia conferta is classified within the plant family Restionaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the restio family and is native to southern Africa. The Restionaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are often found in wetlands and are known for their unique, strap-like leaves. Apterygia conferta is one of the many species within the Restionaceae family, which contains over 600 species. The classification of Apterygia conferta is further broken down into more specific categories, including its genus and species designations.
